en.extract:
The rufous-winged cisticola (Cisticola galactotes) is a species of bird in the family Cisticolidae. It is found on the east coast of southern Africa.


Taxonomy
The rufous-winged cisticola has two subspecies:

C. g. isodactylus Peters, W, 1868 from southern Malawi, south-eastern Zimbabwe and western Mozambique; and
C. g. galactotes (Temminck, 1821) from southern Mozambique and eastern South Africa.
This taxon was split from the winding cisticola by the IOC and HBW, as were the Luapula cisticola, coastal cisticola and Ethiopian cisticola. The Clements (2017) and Howard and Moore (2014) world lists consider these taxa as a single species, the winding cisticola C. galactotes (sensu lato).


Distribution and habitat
It is found in Mozambique, Zimbabwe and South Africa.
Its natural habitats are subtropical or tropical seasonally wet or flooded lowland grassland and swamps.


References


External links

 Data related to Cisticola galactotes at Wikispecies
Black-backed cisticola (A pre-split name for Rufous-winged cisticola) - Species text in The Atlas of Southern African Birds.

The '''rufous-winged cisticola''' ('''''Cisticola galactotes''''') is a species of [[bird]] in the family [[Cisticolidae]]. It is found on the east coast of southern Africa.<ref name="iucn status 20 November 2021" /><ref name="Ryan2017">{{cite web|last1=Ryan|first1=Peter|title=Rufous-winged Cisticola (Cisticola galactotes)|url=http://www.hbw.com/species/rufous-winged-cisticola-cisticola-galactotes|website=Handbook of the Birds of the World Alive|publisher=Lynx Edicions|access-date=21 August 2017|location=Barcelona|language=en}}</ref>

==Taxonomy==
The rufous-winged cisticola has two [[subspecies]]:<ref name="Gill&Donsker2017">{{cite report|last1=Gill|first1=F.|last2=Donsker|first2=D.|title=IOC World Bird List (v 7.3)|date=2017|doi=10.14344/IOC.ML.7.3|url=http://www.worldbirdnames.org/|access-date=21 August 2017|doi-access=free}}</ref>
* ''C. g. isodactylus'' <small>Peters, W, 1868</small> from southern Malawi, south-eastern Zimbabwe and western Mozambique; and
* ''C. g. galactotes'' <small>(Temminck, 1821)</small> from southern Mozambique and eastern South Africa.

This taxon was split from the [[winding cisticola]] by the [[Birds of the World: Recommended English Names|IOC]] and [[HBW and BirdLife International Illustrated Checklist of the Birds of the World|HBW]], as were the [[Luapula cisticola]], [[coastal cisticola]] and [[Ethiopian cisticola]]. The [[The Clements Checklist of Birds of the World|Clements]] (2017) and [[Howard and Moore Complete Checklist of the Birds of the World|Howard and Moore]] (2014) world lists consider these taxa as a single species, the '''winding cisticola''' ''C. galactotes'' (sensu lato).<ref name="AVIBASE">{{avibase|E2D1D214C3313BA5|Cisticola galactotes}}</ref>

==Distribution and habitat==
It is found in [[Mozambique]], [[Zimbabwe]] and [[South Africa]].

Its natural [[habitat]]s are subtropical or tropical seasonally wet or flooded lowland [[grassland]] and [[swamp]]s.
